title: dendromecon harfordii var. rhamnoides
intro: Dendromecon harfordii var. rhamnoides, also known as the bush poppy or California bush poppy, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers. The plant is used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits associated with Dendromecon harfordii var. rhamnoides include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other minor health issues.
